claim: Agent traces are fundamentally different from traditional observability: highly semi-structured with massive unstructured text (gigabytes per trace, 20MB per span vs KB in traditional systems).
actionable: Don't use traditional observability tools (Datadog, Grafana) for agent traces; build or use systems designed for semistructured text (e.g., full-text search indexes).
source_title: How agent o11y differs from traditional o11y

claim: Non-technical domain experts (clinicians, lawyers, wealth advisors) should directly grade agent traces to create training signals; their annotations surface real failure modes.
actionable: Include domain experts in agent evaluation workflows; have them grade trace outputs and justify their scores; use their justifications to train automated scoring functions.
source_title: How agent o11y differs from traditional o11y

claim: Full-text search across agent traces (for any mention of a specific word) is essential but requires custom indexing (Tantivy/Lucene-like); traditional analytics databases lack this.
actionable: When building agent observability, implement full-text indexing alongside structured indices; allow engineers to query all traces mentioning specific terms/concepts.
source_title: How agent o11y differs from traditional o11y

claim: Agent observability and evals are the same problem; the only difference is batch vs real-time execution and whether inputs are known ahead of time.
actionable: Design observability systems that double as eval infrastructure; use the same trace data and scoring for both production monitoring and offline experimentation.
source_title: How agent o11y differs from traditional o11y

claim: Topic modeling on agent traces (clustering by intent/sentiment/issues) helps identify usage patterns and incident detection; high vent volume spikes correlate with platform incidents.
actionable: Use lightweight embeddings + clustering on agent traces to surface usage patterns and anomalies; monitor for sudden increases in trace clusters related to errors.
source_title: How agent o11y differs from traditional o11y
